title: Bus Structure and Arbitration
summary: Explains bus structure, arbitration (centralised vs. decentralised), and electrical implementation details (tri-state, open-collector).
content_points: A bus is a shared communication path with electrical signals and access rules.
Multiple devices may request bus access simultaneously; arbitration grants access.
Centralised: dedicated arbiter circuit decides. Decentralised: devices negotiate.
Tri-state: output can be 0, 1, or high-Z (disconnected); enables bus sharing.
Open-collector: multiple outputs can be wired-AND together safely.
learning_objectives: Explain tri-state and open-collector circuits and their role in bus design
